item.title: ClawWall content sanitization tool — prompt injection defense for all inbound external content
item.intent: Every agent that reads external content (email, web, social media, RSS) must have a sanitization layer that filters prompt injection attacks before content reaches the LLM. This is not optional — it is a security-by-default requirement.
item.description: Clawdius has a battle-tested 'sanitize' tool (ClawWall) that filters all inbound content from X, Tavily, Substack, email, and news through a prompt injection firewall implementing OWASP LLM01 Tier 1+2 defenses. It logs, quarantines, and strips malicious content. ClawHQ has no equivalent — generated tools pass raw external content directly to the LLM context.
steps: Design the sanitize tool API: stdin filter, configurable strictness levels, quarantine log format
Implement sanitize as a generated tool (src/design/tools/sanitize.ts) included in every blueprint by default
Wire all external-facing tool generators (tavily, email, quote) to pipe through sanitize
Add quarantine log: ~/.clawhq/<agent>/security/quarantine.jsonl for blocked content
Add OWASP LLM01 Tier 1 patterns: system prompt overrides, role confusion, instruction injection
Add Tier 2 patterns: encoded payloads (base64, hex), Unicode homoglyphs, invisible characters
Add tests with real-world prompt injection examples
Add doctor check: verify sanitize is on PATH and quarantine log is writable
acceptanceCriteria: A sanitize tool is generated and placed on PATH for every new agent deployment
All external-facing tools (email, tavily, web search) pipe content through sanitize
Known prompt injection patterns (OWASP LLM01 Tier 1+2) are detected and stripped
Quarantine log records all filtered content with timestamps and source attribution
clawhq doctor checks sanitize availability and log writability
Sanitize tool works as a standalone stdin filter (composable with any tool)
